transcript: Hi everyone. Um, Francesco, co-founder and CEO of Almanax. At Almanax, we're building an AI security engineer that can help teams building on Solana ship code without bugs and catch any bugs before bad actors do. We're soon gonna have like trillions of dollars like moving on-chain, from life savings, like real-world assets, and like institutions building products on Solana. Like blockchains are becoming critical infrastructure. Solana is becoming critical infrastructure. Projects on Solana and protocols on Solana are critical infrastructure. And so we need like the best defensive tools we can build to prevent like bad actors to exploiting, from exploiting this critical infrastructure. So, we're soon gonna have like superhuman capabilities in many different fields. And at Almanax, we're building superhuman capabilities for security. We are a team of AI and security researchers based in New York. Uh, my previous company, I investigated some of the largest hacks and exploits that ever happened in crypto. And I grew frustrated because a lot of teams were not taking security uh seriously or they were shipping bugs that company that were well known and companies that like uh like the folks before me like can help catch. Unfortunately, audits are very expensive as well, right? And so having a tool that runs continuously during your PR process is something that we thought was needed. Uh, and ideally having like an auditor available 24/7 available via API. My co-founder was at Coinbase and we built a team um of folks coming from all across the industry. There's a few trends that have been worrying me quite a bit recently, and Vibe Coding is one of these. Like, we entered this age of Vibe Coding where literally like teams are shipping millions of lines of code a day. We have companies like Coinbase and Google that are generating 40, 50% of their code, uh accepted code, uh via AI. Cursor alone, this was like a statistic from April, they're pushing a, uh their users are actually like pushing a billion lines of accepted code a day. That is, those numbers are only gonna grow from here. And so, this unfortunately like produces like massive bottlenecks, right? Because if we're moving towards a a stage where teams are shipping like entire Vibe Coded apps, and I was just talking to uh this uh the co-founder of another security company backstage and they're seeing programs and smart contracts also being Vibe Coded, like who is checking that there's no bugs or there's no malicious code that is being injected or simply buggy code that is making into production? Today like security and engineering teams are literally swamped with like alerts from static analysis tools. Uh, like they do some often off uh fortunately they often do like audits, uh they run bug bounties, like they they have other tools and but like the the massive amount of alerts that they get uh makes it very hard for them to go and like then triage and then pass it to the engineering team to go and actually patch these these issues. And so often, especially in large organizations, like we see that what actually gets patched uh is is not the entirety of what what gets caught, right? Because there's a lot of noise. And so, you guys, how many of you guys have seen the recent Anthropic report that were like were released around security? Quite a few, right? And it's funny because like they released two recently. Like the first one that they released talks about like cyber uh espionage and like a it's a large-scale cyber attacks that they took down from like Chinese state-sponsored hacker where these like bad actors were using AI in their exploit. And then like a week or two weeks after they released another report where they used AI to like exploit smart contracts deployed on-chain. And it's it's getting kind of scary, right? Like these these models are getting to abilities of uh like very thoughtful and very senior like security engineers. We're not there yet, right? Uh, we're like at at this moment we're probably at the level of like junior security engineer, but the tr if the trend continues, we're soon gonna be at superhuman ability when it comes to security as well. And there's some hints around the Balancer hack, 120 million like uh lost recently, where like the attackers might have used AI in their exploits. And so, that's kind of like the main reason why we started Almanax. We wanted to build like the best AI tools from a defensive perspective to teams that are building on-chain. And so Almanax is an AI security engineer that scales with your codebase, uh continuously reviews code, investigates and triages alerts, and help actually patch alerts and security issues at scale. Um, I was supposed to do a demo but like I'll do a few slides so I have the clicker. Uh, we built a system that kind of like reasons through a codebase no matter its complexity like a security engineer would, right? And so in order to do that, there's there's multiple things that you you need to do because like codebases are very large, context windows are small. And so you need to give these uh agents the ability to navigate the codebase, right? And so when we start a scan, and when our customers start a scan, whether they're running on their full repo or they're running in their CI/CD pipelines or PR review process, we create like a threat model of the repo, we look at invariants, we create an abstract an abstract syntax tree, we create like function call a function call graph so that the agents are able to navigate to different parts of the codebase and understand like the logic of certain functions like a security engineer would. And so this runs 24/7 in the PR review process, but it can also like be used as a tool that runs like before an audit, for example. So we work with a lot of teams that uh are about to go and do an audit. We work with auditors as well that are using these tools during like their uh review process. And then we also investigate existing like alerts and help like teams triage. If you're a large organization, uh you're probably running different security t tools and generally what we've seen historically is like 90% of the alerts that teams get are uh false positives. And so you're drowned like um drowning in noise from like these alerts and often they might like dismiss something that isn't really a true a false positive. And so what we developed is this system that kind of like does with the context of the codebase, does an automatic pass of triaging to help prioritize what to fix. And then the very last step is actually going and patching. And so, uh if you have like hundreds of alerts uh or hundreds of vulnerabilities that are found, well, I hope you're it's not a program that you're about to launch on-chain because that would be very worrying. Uh, but if it's like a large like monorepo or large codebase, uh you want to be able to like uh patch at scale. And so back in the days you would like pass, the security team would pass all these alerts to the engineers. Now like you we can patch directly in line if uh a an issue is like an easy one or we create a draft PR. So we're working with like security teams today from like various protocols and like wallet infrastructure team, working with Privy, uh we're working with a bunch of Solana uh programs uh Solana protocols as well. And today, uh want to announce our collaboration with the Solana Foundation. Uh, we uh worked very hard to build an agent that is good at understanding Solana protocols. It's good at understanding Solana programs. It has context on like Anchor, it has context on uh anything that matters when like building something on Solana. And as part of the collaboration, we're providing uh and Solana Foundation is helping us like provide like one year of like uh free audits for projects that are building on Solana. And so this is the moment where you guys clap because like uh this is cool, this is amazing. And with this one, I I'll pause. Um, you can get in touch. You can sign up and if you want to see this directly on your codebase, you don't even need to talk to me. Uh, this is my information but um you can go at app.almanax.ai and you can run like an AI audit. And then we'll send you to some of the fantastic auditors that I see here in the space and in the audience like Runtime Verification. But thank you guys, I'm Francesco, I'll talk to you later.

# MetaMask Announces Free Solana ID Minting for Users Before Breakpoint 2025
MetaMask is offering free Solana ID minting until December 22, unlocking exclusive Solana ecosystem perks for users.
![Kelvin Munene](https://secure.gravatar.com/avatar/d4ea74978aea7ee299447dc24a59aa1dac1fdc2a31a46fbf19da2cdce3d322bd?s=32&r=g)By[Kelvin Munene](https://coincentral.com/author/kelvin/)December 8, 2025**4 Mins Read
![](https://coincentral.com/wp-content/uploads/2017/08/metamask-1.png "MetaMask Announces Free Solana ID Minting for Users Before Breakpoint 2025")
## TLDR
* MetaMask users can mint a Solana ID for free until December 22, 2025.
* The initiative provides users access to perks like staking boosts and rewards.
* Solana partners like Orca and Sanctum offer exclusive benefits for Solana ID holders.
* The program aims to increase wallet adoption and engagement in Solana apps.
MetaMaskhas announced a new initiative that offers users the chance to mint a free Solana ID. This special promotion is available until December 22, 2025. The move is part of a broader partnership between MetaMask and Solana Identity, ahead of Solana’s annual developer conference, Breakpoint 2025. This initiative is designed to encourage users to engage more with Solana’s ecosystem and to provide them with exclusive benefits.
**Mint a Solana ID to Unlock Exclusive Ecosystem Perks**
By minting a Solana ID using MetaMask,[users](https://x.com/MetaMask/status/1998060127950409942?s=20)can unlock various perks across the Solana blockchain ecosystem. These include rewards like staking boosts, exclusive swaps, and access to discounts. Additionally, users will be eligible to participate in raffles and earn rewards from several key partners within the Solana network. Notable Solana-based platforms involved in the program include Orca, SNS, Sanctum, Cudis, and Play Solana.
MetaMask users will not only gain access to these platform-specific rewards but will also be able to explore further opportunities within the Solana ecosystem. These perks are meant to deepen user engagement and increase wallet adoption among MetaMask users who are interacting with Solana applications.
**How the Solana ID Minting Works**
Minting a Solana ID via MetaMask is straightforward. Users simply need to connect their[MetaMask](https://coincentral.com/metamask-introduces-musd-to-simplify-crypto-payments-and-defi-access/)wallet to the Solana Identity platform and follow the steps to mint their Solana ID. The process is free, and users will retain the ID throughout their interactions with Solana-based services. Once minted, the Solana ID will be linked to the user’s MetaMask wallet, allowing them to access the various ecosystem benefits.
The initiative aims to bring together the strengths of MetaMask’s popular walle

sources.title: Metaplex
sources.snippet:
[Back to Blog](https://metaplex.com/blog)

## October Highlights

- **Genesis**\- Genesis powered two presales in October, accounting for 18% of the total monthly revenue, the highest share since inception.

- **Protocol Revenue** \- Protocol revenue reached $2.0M in October.

- **$MPLX Buyback**\- $1.1M of Metaplex protocol fees were used to buy back 5.3M $MPLX (0.5% of total supply / 0.9% of outstanding supply) for the Metaplex DAO.


## Pump.Fun Update (November)

On November 7th, Pump.Fun [disclosed in its developer documentation](https://github.com/pump-fun/pump-public-docs/blob/main/README.md) that it would begin minting tokens using its new Mayhem Mode through SPL Token-22 rather than Metaplex’s Token Metadata. This change went into effect on November 12th and since then the percentage of total tokens launched daily by Pump.Fun that use Metaplex’s Token Metadata has declined to less than 15%.

Metaplex Foundation expects this to result in a decline in Metaplex protocol revenue near term with Pump.Fun contributing 44%, 60%, and 45% of total revenue during August, September, and October, respectively, after peaking at 86% in January 2025.

In light of the slowdown in memecoin activity, over the last few months the Metaplex Foundation has intentionally supported the expansion of the protocol from being purely an asset standard to a broader platform for Internet Capital Markets on Solana. Genesis, Metaplex’s protocol for fair onchain token launches, has found product market fit with over $1.1M in revenue generated since its launch in July 2025 and is now scaling. Metaplex is well positioned to become the canonical end-to-end platform for launching, discovering, and trading new assets on Solana.

The Metaplex Foundation remains well funded with more than three years of operating runway to support the Metaplex ecosystem even accounting for the loss of Pump.Fun associated revenue. This excludes the value of Foundation's and the Metaplex DAO's MPLX positions, which collectively account for more than 40% of the total supply. While this change will have a significant impact on revenue, the concentration risk was well understood, requiring no significant changes in the Metaplex Foundation’s operating plans or capital allocation.

Please note that the October revenue figures that follow in the rest of the newsletter below include revenue from Pump.Fun given the change occurred in November.

## October Protocol Activity

In October, Metaplex generated $2.0M in protocol revenue including $1.6M from Token Metadata (78%), $370K from Genesis (18%), and $72K from Core (4%). Genesis accounted for 18% of total revenue, its highest share since inception.

Combined, token creation (TM tokens) and token launches (Genesis) accounted for 91% of revenue while 9% was driven by the creation of NFTs (TM NFTs, Core, BG v2).

In October, 766K Token Metadata assets were created, including 720K fungible tokens (94%).

Core adoption increased in October, with 238K mints driving the cumulative mint total over 4M.

In October, creators and developers minted over 4M digital assets through Metaplex, including 3.0M compressed NFTs. This activity raised the protocol’s cumulative total to over 960M assets minted to date.

Unique user activity continued in October, with approximately 420K unique wallets authorizing transactions powered by Metaplex.Approximately 290K new wallets collected a Token Metadata asset and over 570K new wallets collected a Bubblegum NFT in the month of October.

## Developer Platform

**Genesis**

October marked another strong month for Genesis, powering multiple token launches and expanding its role across gaming, DeFi, and consumer apps.

[The month began with PlaySolana ($PLAY)](https://x.com/metaplex/status/1977434437748150707), a consumer gaming company building portable hardware devices and an onchain gaming ecosystem. [Their fixed-price presale sold out within minutes, selling over $2.6M (13.2K SOL) to more than 1,600 participant

## Introducing Drift v3: Built to Outperform
December 4, 2025
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6310e7dee49f08e5798eed50/693023e13e4b72ada17e0fe2_2025-12-03%2017.19.13.jpg)
[

Trade Now
](https://app.drift.trade/)
![]()
![]()
Drift v3 is the protocol’s biggest performance upgrade yet. Rebuilt around trader execution, v3 delivers**10x faster fills, 10x improved liquidity, and a cleaner, more intuitive interface**than v2.
This upgrade was built for one thing: outperformance. Instant execution. Deeper liquidity. A suite of tools that helps you trade faster and with more confidence.
Drift v2 was the first to bring cross-margin and multi-asset collateral to DeFi on-chain trading. We built on Solana to deliver NASDAQ-level performance on-chain and with major Solana upgrades like Alpenglow, Firedancer and Jito’s Block Assembly Market (BAM), Drift is built to outperform as the network scales.
Since launch, Drift has surpassed 1.5B in TVL, $133B+ in total trading volume and $650M in open interest. Drift v3 is built to take those numbers and your trading even further.
**Drift v3 will be available on Dec 4, 2025.**New users will need an invite code to access Drift v3.
**DM**[**@DriftProtocol**](https://x.com/DriftProtocol)**to request access.**
## Why v3?
Drift v3 represents the protocol’s next stage of maturity: the point where years of infrastructure, liquidity, and execution improvements converge into a faster, clearer, more reliable trading experience.
**Over the past year, Drift has evolved in three critical areas:**
1. Execution speed
2. Liquidity depth
3. Trader experience## How Drift v3 outperforms
*The road to v3 was focused on improving liquidity, trading execution and speed. Here’s what that looks like in practice:*
### **Trading Execution and Speed**
* **10x faster order fills:**On Drift v3, 85% of market orders are filled within the same slot. A slot is Solana’s smallest time unit (about 400 milliseconds). On Drift, that means that trades are executed in under half a second. Instant execution.
* **15x faster take profit/stop loss triggers:**For BTC, it dropped from 15 slots (\~6 seconds) to 1 slot (\~0.4 seconds). This means your risk controls and exits now trigger immediately, reducing slippage and missed opportunities.
* **Gasless trading by default:**Unlike other DEXs, where you pay gas on every trade, Drift abstracts gas fees so that’s one less thing to worry about eating into profits
* **400ms oracle updates**: Prices update every 400 millisecond (roughly 2.5 times per second). This keeps quotes accurate and reduces the chance of stale pricing.
* **Scales with Solana:**As Solana increases block size and throughput, Drift automatically gets faster too, handling more trades per second.### **Liquidity**
* **10x reduction in slippage on market orders:**In Drift v2, large market orders could move the price by 20 bps. In v3, that’s been cut to just 2 bps. Traders now get far better pricing, especially on size.
* **5x tighter TP/SL execution:**Take-profit and stop-loss orders are far more precise. On major markets like SOL and BTC, fills are 4x tighter, while non-majors see up to 5x improvement. This means exits trigger closer to the price you set, reducing losses and protecting gains.
* **Introduction of Drift Liquidity Provider (DLP)**: Provides liquidity for both Perps and Spot markets. Community members can deposit funds into DLP to support market-making and earn a share of the pool's profits. Currently in the testing stage. Public launch in Q1 2026.
* **Expanded Market Maker Program:**With v3’s expanded market maker initiatives, long-tail altcoins now hav

editorial.headline: Solana Foundation Backs Almanax to Automate Ecosystem Security
editorial.standfirst: As 'vibe coding' floods repositories with AI-generated scripts, manual auditing creates dangerous bottlenecks for institutional protocols. A new subsidy program aims to embed autonomous security engineers directly into the CI/CD pipeline of Solana projects.
key_takeaways: **Solana Foundation** partnership provides one year of free AI audits for ecosystem projects.
**Almanax** processes code using abstract syntax trees and threat modeling to simulate human security engineering.
The initiative counters the rise of 'vibe coding,' where platforms like **Cursor** now push over 1 billion lines of accepted code daily.
editorial.article_body_markdown:
The rapid adoption of generative AI in software development has introduced a critical asymmetry in blockchain security. With major tech firms like **Google** and **Coinbase** now generating nearly half of their accepted code via AI, and **Cursor** users pushing billions of lines daily, the industry has entered the era of "vibe coding." While this accelerates shipping velocity, it overwhelms traditional security teams, creating a target-rich environment for state-sponsored actors who are simultaneously leveraging AI to identify vulnerabilities in smart contracts.

**Almanax** addresses this bottleneck by deploying an "AI Security Engineer" that integrates directly into the development lifecycle. Unlike traditional static analysis tools that drown developers in false positives, Almanax utilizes a sophisticated reasoning engine. It constructs threat models, analyzes invariants, and maps abstract syntax trees (ASTs) to navigate complex codebases with the logic of a human auditor. This allows the system to not only flag vulnerabilities during the Pull Request (PR) process but also draft patches automatically, effectively closing the loop between detection and remediation.

The newly announced collaboration with the **Solana Foundation** represents a strategic hardening of the network's infrastructure. By subsidizing one year of free AI audits for builders, the Foundation is lowering the barrier to entry for institutional-grade security. This is particularly vital as the threat landscape evolves; recent reports from **Anthropic** highlight how AI agents are already being used to exploit on-chain logic. As Solana positions itself as critical global infrastructure for real-world assets and institutional capital, shifting from periodic manual audits to continuous, autonomous defense is no longer optional—it is a prerequisite for survival.
